goal: 调整 phase-1 conflict 检测范围：只检查 active proposed 和当前 base 之后 merge 的 intent

summary.title: 收敛 merged intent 的 phase-1 冲突范围
summary.what: Seal-time phase-1 conflict detection now checks all proposed intents, but only checks merged intents whose merged main commit landed after the current intent's base commit. Existing merged history that is already included in the base is no longer treated as a default conflict source. The agent guidance template and agent context protocol spec now describe the same base-scoped behavior.
summary.why: Merged intents can still matter when another agent's proposed work lands on main while the current intent is in progress, but checking every historical merged intent turns high-traffic files such as README.md into noisy conflict lists. Scoping merged intent eligibility by base..main keeps the race protection while removing old history from the active conflict surface.
summary.user_goal: 调整 phase-1 conflict 检测范围：只检查 active proposed 和当前 base 之后 merge 的 intent

decisions.chose: Use the current intent's base commit and synced main head to build a base..main commit window, then include only merged intents whose merged_main_commit is inside that window.
decisions.rationale: Git ancestry is more precise than a time window and naturally captures the case where someone else's intent merged after this work began.

decisions.chose: Preserve the old conservative merged-intent behavior when base or ancestry cannot be determined.
decisions.rationale: If Mainline cannot prove which merged commits are already in the base, it should avoid silently missing a real recent upstream merge.

decisions.chose: Keep all proposed intents eligible for seal-time phase-1 conflict detection.
decisions.rationale: Proposed intents represent active or unterminaled concurrent work and remain the primary collaboration conflict surface.

rejected.reason: That would miss the race where another agent's overlapping proposed intent merges while the current branch is still based on an older main.

rejected.alternative: Check only recently merged intents by wall-clock time
rejected.reason: Time windows do not match Git causality; base..main directly answers whether the merged work is already in the candidate's base.

review_notes: Validated with focused engine tests for scoped conflict eligibility, conservative unknown-scope fallback, seal-submit race coverage, and existing conflict properties.
Did not run make test, per repository guidance.

architectural_claims: Seal-time phase-1 conflict detection treats active proposed intents and post-base merged intents as current conflict material.
Historical merged intents already reachable from the current intent base are retrieval context, not default conflict warnings.
When ancestry scope cannot be computed, the detector falls back to conservative merged-intent coverage.
behavioral_changes: seal --submit now computes a merged conflict scope from draft.base_commit to the synced main head before running phase-1 detection.
Merged intents with merged_main_commit outside that base..main window no longer appear in seal-time conflicts.
Proposed intents remain eligible for seal-time conflict warnings.
